items.title: LexisNexis confirms data breach as hackers leak stolen files
items.description: American data analytics company LexisNexis Legal & Professional has confirmed to BleepingComputer that hackers breached its servers and accessed some customer and business information. [...]

items.title: Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability
items.description: The researcher says he has identified thousands of internet-exposed IQ4 building management controllers. The post Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Compromised Site Management Panels are a Hot Item in Cybercrime Markets
items.description: Compromised cPanel credentials are being sold in bulk across underground channels as plug-and-play phishing and scam infrastructure. Flare explains how analyzing 200,000 underground posts reveals a commoditized market for hacked site management panels. [...]

items.title: New ‘AirSnitch’ Attack Shows Wi-Fi Client Isolation Could Be a False Sense of Security
items.description: Researchers have uncovered a Wi-Fi vulnerability that allows nearby attackers to intercept sensitive data and execute machine-in-the-middle attacks against connected devices. The post New ‘AirSnitch’ Attack Shows Wi-Fi Client Isolation Could Be a False Sense of Security appeared first on

items.title: 1.2 Million Affected by University of Hawaii Cancer Center Data Breach
items.description: Hackers stole names, Social Security numbers, driver’s license information, voter registration records, and health-related information. The post 1.2 Million Affected by University of Hawaii Cancer Center Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Android Update Patches Exploited Qualcomm Zero-Day
items.description: An integer overflow or wraparound in the Qualcomm graphics component, the bug leads to memory corruption. The post Android Update Patches Exploited Qualcomm Zero-Day appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
items.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. items.title: Star Citizen game dev discloses breach affecting user data
items.description: Cloud Imperium Games (CIG), the game developer behind Star Citizen and Squadron 42, says attackers breached systems containing some users' personal information in January. [...]

items.title: Vulnerability in MS-Agent AI Framework Can Allow Full System Compromise
items.description: Improper input sanitization in the framework can be exploited through the Shell tool, allowing attackers to modify system files and steal data. The post Vulnerability in MS-Agent AI Framework Can Allow Full System Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UH Cancer Center data breach affects nearly 1.2 million people
items.description: The University of Hawaii confirmed that a ransomware gang stole the data of nearly 1.2 million individuals in August 2025 after breaching its Cancer Center's Epidemiology Division. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
items.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company said, targets government and public-sector organizations with the end goal of redire

items.title: Android gets patches for Qualcomm zero-day exploited in attacks
items.description: Google has released security updates to patch 129 Android security vulnerabilities, including an actively exploited zero-day flaw in a Qualcomm display component. [...]

items.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
items.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21385 (CVSS score: 7.8), a buffer over-read in the Graphics component. "Memory corruption when a

items.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
items.description: Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. [...]

items.title: Fake Google Security site uses PWA app to steal credentials, MFA codes
items.description: A phishing campaign is using a fake Google Account security page to deliver a web-based app capable of stealing one-time passcodes, harvesting cryptocurrency wallet addresses, and proxying attacker traffic through victims' browsers. [...]

items.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0628 (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as a case of ins

items.title: Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant
items.description: Malicious extensions could hijack the Gemini Live in Chrome feature to spy on users and steal their files. The post Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: How Deepfakes and Injection Attacks Are Breaking Identity Verification
items.description: Deepfakes and injection attacks are targeting identity verification moments, from onboarding to account recovery. Incode explains why enterprises must validate the full session—media, device integrity, and behavior—to stop synthetic and injected attacks in real time. [...]

items.title: How to Protect Your SaaS from Bot Attacks with SafeLine WAF
items.description: Most SaaS teams remember the day their user traffic started growing fast. Few notice the day bots started targeting them. items.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai.
